channel.title: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA"

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Username: Hollows

Experience: I am an IT professional with 4 years of experience in system administration, technical support, and information management. Experience maintaining 24/7/365 and/or 100% availability systems for local and state governments, in addition to creating custom solutions for customers using a variety of technologies depending on the customer's needs.

Professional Experience: I have experience in a number of IT roles, almost all with government systems. One of my previous roles consisted of primarily tier one IT support, including customer support, router ans switch maintenance, 0365 and Exchange maintenance, server administration, AP installation, VLAN configuration, etc. Another role was in IT project management, converting outdated organization frameworks to modern ITIL, ServiceNow, and Jira frameworks where applicable.

My most recent role included all aforementioned tier one duties with the addition of some IT management, including creating additions to the business continuity plan and disaster recovery plan. In this role, I also managed certain parts of FOIA requests, maintaining content that should be available to the public 100% of the time to comply with government oversight and transparency law.

Technical documentation is a specialty of mine. I have been hired before based solely on the quality of my documentation and technical writing.

Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: bachelor's degree in Information Technology w/ magna cum laude honors from my program. Was president of local association of IT professionals for two years, expert with networking and coordination.

What technologies are you familiar with in your area?: I am familiar with a myriad of resource management and issue tracking systems, such as ServiceNow, Jira, KACE, and custom tracking solutions based on O365 SharePoint and WordPress. Experience with Docker, PowerShell, Bash, Kubernetes, and VMWare for the fully automated creation, configuration, and implementation of containerized VMs with 0 downtime. Creation and maintenance of containerized full-stack Elasticsearch-Logstash-Kibana data collection and visualization system.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
• Username: Proteus Steinmetz
  • Applying For: System Administration
  • Experience: If this site truly requires a proper system administrator, then I can assist the SCP Wiki team in this field. I have about 8 years of experience in system administration, and I am currently a full-time penetration tester and cybersecurity researcher. As a system administrator, I have experience across various operating systems (Solaris, Red Hat Linux, Windows Server, MacOS, etc.) and software management suites. I also have experience with servers, IT security systems, and other critical components of an organization's IT infrastructure. I also have a strong background in system automation and virtualization.
  • Professional Experience: I have experience managing critical infrastructure for the Department of Defense, the Federal Aviation Administration, and the United States Air Force. I was in charge of the primary and secondary networks that handled real-time air traffic control operations for the FAA at multiple sites. In the Air Force, I was the lead member of the radar network responsible for providing weather and air traffic data to both military and civilian satellite sites. For the Department of Defense, I managed thousands of classified machines, intrusion detection systems, and user accounts as well as sensitive proprietary applications.
  • Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skillset: Bachelor's in Information Technology with a security focus, CompTIA Linux+, CompTIA Network+, CompTIA Security+, OSCP, OSCE, Certified Ethical Hacker v10, Penetration Testing Professional v5, Certified Information Systems Security Professional, Certified Information Systems Auditor, Coined and awarded an Air Force achievement medal by Chief of Staff General Mark A. Welsh III of the United States Air Force for identifying and designing a fix action for a significant network design flaw, AFSC Oustanding Airman of the Year across the global Air Force talent pool, Awarded SrA Below-The-Zone, Diamond Sharp Award, Air Force Air Mobility Command Inspector General's Outstanding Performer Award
  • What technologies are you familiar with within your area of expertise? For instance, do you use Jira to track QA issues? Do you work with JUnit for Unit tests? Ever used Docker? Tell me about your abilities here: JIRA is fine enough, the government uses its own proprietary system, but the only issue with JIRA is if you have someone set it up poorly. The program also handles emails pretty wonky. My last thing to note about it is that it gets expensive quickly as the user pool increases for licenses. For unit testing, I enjoy TestNG over JUnit since it has a bit more functions available but JUnit is fine too. I prefer to use Kubernetes in general, but it also works well alongside Docker. I have a lot of automation experience since in sysadmin that's the name of the game. I love bash and batch scripting but a few languages I have direct experience with are SQL, C, C++, Perl, Python, C#, Java, Powershell, and .NET as a technology in general. On the cybersecurity side of the house, I employ Kali Linux as most do, with BurpSuite and Metasploit.

Overall, I've pretty much used most of the standard sysadmin tools over the years and can learn anything new quickly based on prior experience.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Username: ManyMeats

Experience: I am an IT professional with 12 years of experience in systems administration and technical support. Previous jobs include 'Solutions Engineer' for a managed services firm, 'Network Engineer' for a Fortune 500 company in their network operations center, a 'Storage and Backup Engineer' for a US leader in insurance, and most recently full system support for a State agency in Wisconsin. During this time, I have accumulated a tremendous level of exposure to different technologies including more than half of my professional career building, supporting, and maintaining 24/7/365 systems in a data center or hosted environment.

Professional Experience: I would list myself as "advanced" in the following items: Windows Server 2008-2016, Office 365, Citrix VDI-in-a-box, VMWare (especially NTX and Horizons), Commvault backup and recovery, Hitachi and NetApp SANS, SQL Server, Powershell, and Cisco switches.

I would consider myself "intermediate" or to have tertiary experience with the following items: RedHat (5, 6, 7), Oracle (11, 12), Veeam, Adaptive firewall appliances such as Cisco ASAs Palo Alto devices, racking and cabling, cable termination, and other general data center tasks. I'm sure there are other miscellaneous things I could probably list.

Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: I have a Bachelors in Management Information Systems and a Bachelors in Business Administration (w/ minor in CompSci). I have the following vendor-specific accreditations: Commvault Certified Engineer, VMware Certified Professional, CCNA-Routing&Switching, A+ Certification.

What technologies are you familiar with in your area?: I am familiar with a number of issue and ticket tracking items such as Spiceworks, ServiceNow, and Numera Footprints. In the IT world, fast adaptation to things like this is necessary for survival. Additionally, I do a lot of cross-work with application developers, so I am familiar with github and Jira for iteration and issue tracking, however I am not a frequent end-user for these softwares myself.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if your still accepting people but i feel i could help with finding bigs and errors n stuff.

Username: Vulcov
Experience: i like breaking things and finding where they don't work. I've done it before for video/computer games for a couple friends in the past and helped test some website features a couple years back for a buddy.
Technologies: well… i have a baseball bat… no but seriously I have a computer and an obsessive compulsion to learn just tell me what you'll need point me in the right direction and i'll see what I can do.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
I have a hosting bussines i can provide SCP for free.
www.lantania.net
Let me know and i give unlimited resources!

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Username: Aschakida

Applying for: Testing, especially searching for errors, trying to trick the system and searching for systematical errors.

Experience: You will receive a PM with more details. I am uncomfortable telling this in the public.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded: Cross-posting over here — I have experience in both development and server administration.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded: Awesome, I'll note that down. Thank you.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded: Just thought I'd add as well that unless someone else more qualified pops up, I am happy to act as an eventual support manager if required. I have acted as a temporary team leader before however that hasn't been my official job (yet(hopefully). I haven't put this in the management thread as I don't feel it qualifies but I am familiar with managing a support team.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded: I was going to just paste in my resume but I thought better of it since editing my resume at work to include the name "djkaktus" might be viewed suspiciously.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
I'll mark you down as a QA tester under whoever eventually coordinates.

Thank you for your interest!

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
I'll mark you down as a QA tester under whoever eventually coordinates.

Thank you for your interest!

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Damn two crazy qualified people at once. It's christmas.

Added to the skills sheet, thank you for your interest!

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Oh Kaktus, what big qualifications you have~…

Added to the sheet, and thank you for your interest!

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Username: djkaktus

Experience: IRL, I'm a QA Coordinator/Supervisor for a billion-dollar company out of Atlanta. Most of the work I do concerns meeting product specifications for our customers, as well as setting material specifications with our suppliers and working with end users to find acceptable solutions to issues they are having with their product. I singlehandedly process, approve, and resolve upwards on $100K worth of claims each week.

At the same time, I also help manage our QA department, including training, hands-on development of testing protocols, and working with our product development engineers to create new technologies in our field while still meeting customer standards and safety regulations. I additionally manage requisitions for both myself and my department, frequently managing thousands of dollars worth of materials and supplies each week.

Lastly, I'm also the Lead Auditor for our ISO Team, and am in charge of making sure our facility meets all of the standards of ISO 9001. In doing so, I also act as our Plant Management Representative, working with our customers and suppliers to meet required regulatory standards, etc. I also help lead our Food Safety Team, as well as our Job Safety Initiative and Social Outreach teams.

Professional Experience: See above. TL;DR: Quality Management.

Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: ISO 9001 certification, ISO 22000 certification, OSHA certification, various food and job safety certifications, etc.

What technologies are you familiar with in your area? Admittedly my IT experience is minimal at best, and since my experience is almost completely devoted to manufacturing my best talents worth bringing to the table are in QA personnel management and supervisory leadership, unless you're planning on managing the project on Oracle's JD Edwards E1 in which case are we actually building a website or are we instead getting into plastics.

That being said, we manage our QA process on about a half dozen different systems, each of which I've had to become proficient with in a relatively short period of time, so I'm sure we could make it work.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
I didn't even realise there was a support role, count me in.

Applying for support/sysadmin

Username: ChrisAKAPiefish

Experience: 3 and a half years in IT support, supported Citrix, Kaspersky, Sophos, Office 2010-2014, general Server/user support and then 4 years in a service desk job where I've supported 5 different bits of software, 3 using SQL, 2 being OMNIS based.

Professional Experience: Currently working in a service desk job where I support currently silverlight (yuck) based software with a SQL database in the back end. We'll be heading over to HMTL 5 shortly as well. As I'm currently working in support I do also have experience of support issues.

Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: BTEC in IT Practitioners but mostly just experience. I do also have access to Pluralsight for personal development training.

What technologies are you familiar with in your area? For instance, do you use Jira to track QA issues? Do you work with JUnit for Unit tests? Ever used Docker? Tell me about your abilities here: Have use JIRA at previous job, currently using VSTS. Ticketing softwares include Salesforce and LANDesk, I have also used Spiceworks before and was a very active member on its community, regally use SSMS, have experience using crystal reports, current software uses IIS (I wouldn't say I'm an expert but I am familiar with IIS), familiar with Active Directory and general windows domain tools.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Applying for Quality Assurance/Tester

Username: Moist Spaghetti

Experience: I have a natural ability to break almost everything I come into contact with, both digitally and physically. From websites to triple A game titles to apps, I've found more bugs than a 60 year old entomologist.

Professional Experience: Currently working with a few teams on small projects, acting as a QA tester. I think that counts as professional.

Technologies: Have not used any dedicated QA software, but willing to learn.

item.title: Re: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Username: Dr_Grom

Applying for: Quality Assurance and testing from the view of a nonprofessional user and admin. Also for pestering you with unrequested opinions. Can provide a dedicated server for testing (the one KIRA is running on)

Experience: Administration, creation and modification of several Wikidot Wikis and other older, rather unprofessional webprojects before I joined the Foundation. Also beta-tested several story mods for TES: Oblivion a century ago or so.

Professional experience: I am an engineer, developing and designing dust extraction systems and working booths for several industry sectors. Including systems for hazardous and explosive material. I am trained to forsee what can go wrong and prevent it at the earliest point. I also have an eye for irregularities and can give very detailed reports.

item.title: Recruitment Thread - Systems Administration, Support, and QA
item.content:encoded:
Greetings Skippers.

As pointed out in my project plan the next task to accomplish is the gathering of resources.

In order to do that, we're posting a series of threads to figure out who we have available to use, resource wise, and what skills they possess.

Some disclaimers before you post:

  1. All work done for Project Foundation is on a volunteer basis, and you should not expect, and are not entitled to, compensation of any kind.
  2. Completing work for Project Foundation does not entitle you to any Staff Role or authority on the wiki, or any eventual platform.
  3. If you volunteer your skills you accept that you will most likely have to work under a manager who will assign you tasks to perform. You are free to make suggestions to your manager; however at the end of the day, they will likely have the final say on the direction of your work.
  4. Working for Project Foundation will require a minimum commitment to producing work within a reasonable timeframe as assigned by the project manager, or your manager. Your manager will work with your schedule but ultimately decides expectations on task completion dates.

All of that being said, this post is a call for: Software Support, Systems Administration, or Quality Assurance.

We are looking for people who can hit the ground running with this area, and be able to perform the required tasks without significant instruction or hand-holding.

If you feel you have these skills, and would like to collaborate on Project Foundation, please respond below with the following responses:

  • Username: <Your Wiki Username>
  • Experience: <Describe your experience level with Sysadmin, QA, or Support and how you can contribute to Project Foundation in this area. Please be as detailed as you feel comfortable being, as this will let us know what area to assign you to.>
  • Professional Experience: <Respond here with what professional experience you have with these skills. >
  • Any professional accolades, certifications, or qualifications for this skill set: <Self Explanatory>
  • What technologies are you familiar with in your area? For instance, do you use Jira to track QA issues? Do you work with JUnit for Unit tests? Ever used Docker? Tell me about your abilities here:

FAQ

FAQ
Hide

Q. Magnus, I have this other skill that I can…
A. Cool, post in the relevant thread.

Q. So how will I know I'm on the team?
A. If the management team feels your skills are applicable, we'll put your name on a big ol' spreadsheet of skills and talents. You'll get a wiki PM from someone on the team to coordinate.

Q. Awesome, when do I get work to do?
A. Eventually. We need to figure out what exactly needs to get done, before anything can get done.

Q. I'm super awesome at bash. I write all my own scripts, and I'm a l33t h@xx0r…
A. That is….cool. What I need are people who are versed in toolsets, administrative practices, and skills of that variety. If it was just writing scripts, that'd be more the programming section. Systems Administration and QA (while not perfectly in sync) are very different skill sets from programmers usually. I don't want to confuse the two.
